title: acmella pilosa
intro: Acmella pilosa, also known as toothache plant, is a tropical plant native to the Americas, characterized by its small white flowers and unique ability to numb the mouth. The plant's leaves and stems contain a compound called spilanthol, which has been found to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential treatment for toothaches and other pain-related issues. The medicinal properties of Acmella pilosa have been used to alleviate toothaches, reduce swelling, and soothe irritated mucous membranes, providing relief from dental pain and inflammation. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal benefits.
